# Generative Artifact Protocol

> Open protocol and Go implementation for token-efficient artifact generation and updates by LLMs. Producers emit JSON envelopes targeting `<gap:target>` markers or JSON Pointers; the stateless Go apply engine resolves them without regenerating the whole artifact.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Installation, quick start, commands, and package overview
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Architecture, commands, and conventions for AI agents
- [GAP specification](spec/gap.md): Normative wire format, envelopes, conformance levels
- [GAP-SSE binding](spec/gap-sse.md): SSE transport binding for streaming envelopes
- [Experiment methodology](assets/evals/experiments/EXPERIMENT.md): Eval dataset layout and validity gates
- [SAIGE eval set](assets/evals/saige/README.md): Generated SAIGE observations for the GAP corpus

## Key Files

- [types.go](types.go): Envelope, artifact, and edit-op wire types
- [apply.go](apply.go): Stateless `Apply(base, envelope)` implementation
- [markers.go](markers.go): `<gap:target>` marker resolution
- [store.go](store.go): Versioned in-memory artifact store
- [cmd/gap-eval](cmd/gap-eval/): Live OpenAI-compatible eval runner
- [internal/liveeval](internal/liveeval/): Provider client, flow runner, metrics writer
- [evalset](evalset/): Loader and committed-metrics scorers for `github.com/urmzd/saige/eval`
- [observations.json](assets/evals/saige/observations.json): Generated SAIGE observation set
- [artifact envelope schema](spec/schemas/artifact-envelope.json): JSON Schema for the envelope wire format
- [results.md](assets/evals/experiments/results.md): Historical aggregated measurements
